contest_compliant_note: GHA fork ubuntu-latest runner image (ubuntu-24.04 / 20260413.86) is BIT-IDENTICAL to upstream commaai/comma_video_compression_challenge eval.yml runner image; cross-verified via run 25391224060 on 2026-05-05.

comparison_with_cuda.interpretation: CPU eval improves PoseNet by 4.86x and SegNet by 1.17x vs CUDA T4 on bit-identical archive bytes. CPU produces a CONSISTENTLY LOWER (better) score because the contest scorers (FastViT-T12 PoseNet attention softmax + EfficientNet-B2 SegNet stem) compute slightly different float values on x86_64 SSE/AVX vs CUDA float16. The leaderboard ranks by CPU eval, so CPU is the medal-band axis.

medal_band_analysis.verdict: BORDERLINE-MEDAL: PR107 CPU 0.1966 lands ~0.0013 above PR102 silver 0.19538. Just outside the top-3 medal band of [0.193, 0.196] by ~1.3 milliscore. Effectively tied with the silver/bronze cluster on the leaderboard's CPU axis.
medal_band_analysis.implication: Our prior CUDA-only score (0.22936) misrepresented PR107's actual leaderboard position by 0.033 score units. The structural CPU/CUDA score gap is ~17% on this archive, dominated by the 4.86x pose drift. Future submissions MUST run BOTH CPU + CUDA auth eval; the CPU score is the operationally relevant medal-band number.

report_text:
=== Evaluation config ===
  batch_size: 16
  device: cpu
  num_threads: 2
  prefetch_queue_depth: 4
  report: submissions/apogee/report.txt
  seed: 1234
  submission_dir: submissions/apogee
  uncompressed_dir: /home/runner/work/comma_video_compression_challenge/comma_video_compression_challenge/videos
  video_names_file: /home/runner/work/comma_video_compression_challenge/comma_video_compression_challenge/public_test_video_names.txt
=== Evaluation results over 600 samples ===
  Average PoseNet Distortion: 0.00003580
  Average SegNet Distortion: 0.00058931
  Submission file size: 178,392 bytes
  Original uncompressed size: 37,545,489 bytes
  Compression Rate: 0.00475136
  Final score: 100*segnet_dist + √(10*posenet_dist) + 25*rate = 0.20
